comments: bigintToDecimal(x) returns the exact decimal representation of x. Since decimal is arbitrary-precision and bigint is also arbitrary-precision, the conversion is lossless.
Total.
metadata.description: Convert a bigint to a decimal.

comments: bigintToInt16(x) returns x reduced modulo 2^16 and reinterpreted as a signed two's-complement int16. Values outside [-2^15, 2^15-1] wrap silently with no exception.
Total.
metadata.description: Convert a bigint to an int16 (truncating).

comments: bigintToInt32(x) returns x reduced modulo 2^32 and reinterpreted as a signed two's-complement int32. Values outside [-2^31, 2^31-1] wrap silently with no exception.
Total.
metadata.description: Convert a bigint to an int32 (truncating).

comments: bigintToInt64(x) returns x reduced modulo 2^64 and reinterpreted as a signed two's-complement int64. Values outside [-2^63, 2^63-1] wrap silently with no exception.
Total.
metadata.description: Convert a bigint to an int64 (truncating).

comments: bigintToInt8(x) returns x reduced modulo 2^8 and reinterpreted as a signed two's-complement int8. Values outside [-128, 127] wrap silently with no exception.
Total.
metadata.description: Convert a bigint to an int8 (truncating).

comments: bigintToUint16(x) returns x reduced modulo 2^16 as an unsigned uint16. Values outside [0, 2^16-1] wrap silently with no exception.
Total.
metadata.description: Convert a bigint to a uint16 (truncating).

comments: bigintToUint32(x) returns x reduced modulo 2^32 as an unsigned uint32. Values outside [0, 2^32-1] wrap silently with no exception.
Total.
metadata.description: Convert a bigint to a uint32 (truncating).

comments: bigintToUint64(x) returns x reduced modulo 2^64 as an unsigned uint64. Values outside [0, 2^64-1] wrap silently with no exception.
Total.
metadata.description: Convert a bigint to a uint64 (truncating).

comments: bigintToUint8(x) returns x reduced modulo 2^8 as an unsigned uint8. Values outside [0, 255] wrap silently with no exception.
Total.
metadata.description: Convert a bigint to a uint8 (truncating).

comments: binaryToBytes(b) returns the bytes of b as an int32 list with values in [0, 255], preserving byte order.
The result list element type is int32 rather than uint8 because Hydra's primitive collections work most naturally with int32 indices.
Total.
metadata.description: Convert binary data to a list of byte values.

comments: binaryToString(b) interprets the bytes of b as a UTF-8-encoded string and returns the decoded value.
The behavior on invalid UTF-8 byte sequences is host-defined: most hosts substitute the replacement character U+FFFD; some may signal an error or truncate.
Total in the sense that it does not raise from the kernel's perspective, but the result may carry the host's replacement semantics.
metadata.description: Convert binary data to a UTF-8 string.

comments: decimalToBigint(x) returns x truncated toward zero. Fractional parts are discarded.
Total.
metadata.description: Convert a decimal to a bigint (truncating).

comments: decimalToFloat32(x) returns the IEEE 754 binary32 value closest to x under roundTiesToEven. Values outside the float32 representable range overflow to ±∞. Subnormal precision loss is silent.
Total.
metadata.description: Convert a decimal to a float32.

comments: decimalToFloat64(x) returns the IEEE 754 binary64 value closest to x under roundTiesToEven. Values outside the float64 representable range overflow to ±∞. Subnormal precision loss is silent.
Total.
metadata.description: Convert a decimal to a float64.

comments: float32ToDecimal(x) returns the exact decimal representation of the binary32 value x. Special float32 values (±∞, NaN) have a host-defined decimal representation; finite values convert exactly since binary32 values are dyadic rationals.
Total.
metadata.description: Convert a float32 to a decimal.

comments: float32ToFloat64(x) returns the binary64 value with the same numeric value as x; ±∞ and NaN pass through. The conversion is exact and lossless.
Total.
metadata.description: Convert a float32 to a float64.

comments: float64ToDecimal(x) returns the exact decimal representation of the binary64 value x. Special float64 values (±∞, NaN) have a host-defined decimal representation; finite values convert exactly since binary64 values are dyadic rationals.
Total.
metadata.description: Convert a float64 to a decimal.

comments: float64ToFloat32(x) returns the binary32 value closest to x under roundTiesToEven. Values outside the binary32 range overflow to ±∞; subnormal precision loss is silent; NaN payload may be canonicalized by the host.
Total.
metadata.description: Convert a float64 to a float32 (lossy).

comments: int16ToBigint(x) returns the bigint with the same numeric value as x. The conversion is exact and lossless.
Total.
metadata.description: Convert an int16 to a bigint.

comments: int32ToBigint(x) returns the bigint with the same numeric value as x. The conversion is exact and lossless.
Total.
metadata.description: Convert an int32 to a bigint.

comments: int64ToBigint(x) returns the bigint with the same numeric value as x. The conversion is exact and lossless.
Total.
metadata.description: Convert an int64 to a bigint.

comments: int8ToBigint(x) returns the bigint with the same numeric value as x. The conversion is exact and lossless.
Total.
metadata.description: Convert an int8 to a bigint.

comments: readBigint(s) returns Just(x) where x is the bigint parsed from s, or Nothing if s is not a valid bigint literal.

Accepted syntax follows the Haskell Show/Read convention: an optional leading minus sign followed by a sequence of decimal digits. Whitespace is not stripped; leading or trailing whitespace causes parse failure.
Total.
metadata.description: Parse a string as a bigint.

comments: readBoolean(s) returns Just(true) for "true", Just(false) for "false", and Nothing for any other input (including "True"/"False" with capital initial letter on some hosts; behavior on capitalized forms is host-defined and should not be relied upon).
Total.
metadata.description: Parse a string as a boolean.

comments: readDecimal(s) returns Just(x) where x is the decimal parsed from s, or Nothing on parse failure.
Accepted syntax is the standard decimal literal: an optional sign, an integer part, an optional fractional part, and an optional exponent (e.g. -1.5, 2e10, 0.0).
Total.
metadata.description: Parse a string as a decimal.

comments: readFloat32(s) returns Just(x) where x is the IEEE 754 binary32 value closest to the number parsed from s under roundTiesToEven, or Nothing on parse failure.
Accepted special-value literals ("NaN", "Infinity", "-Infinity", etc.) and their capitalization are host-defined.
Total.
metadata.description: Parse a string as a float32.

comments: readFloat64(s) returns Just(x) where x is the IEEE 754 binary64 value closest to the number parsed from s under roundTiesToEven, or Nothing on parse failure.
Accepted special-value literals ("NaN", "Infinity", "-Infinity", etc.) and their capitalization are host-defined.
Total.
metadata.description: Parse a string as a float64.

comments: readInt16(s) returns Just(x) where x is the int16 parsed from s, or Nothing if s is not a valid integer literal or the parsed value is outside [-2^15, 2^15-1].
Total.
metadata.description: Parse a string as an int16.

comments: readInt32(s) returns Just(x) where x is the int32 parsed from s, or Nothing if s is not a valid integer literal or the parsed value is outside [-2^31, 2^31-1].
Total.
metadata.description: Parse a string as an int32.

comments: readInt64(s) returns Just(x) where x is the int64 parsed from s, or Nothing if s is not a valid integer literal or the parsed value is outside [-2^63, 2^63-1].
Total.
metadata.description: Parse a string as an int64.

comments: readInt8(s) returns Just(x) where x is the int8 parsed from s, or Nothing if s is not a valid integer literal or the parsed value is outside [-128, 127].
Total.
metadata.description: Parse a string as an int8.

comments: readString(s) parses s as a Haskell-syntax string literal token: an opening double-quote, escaped characters per the Haskell lexical grammar (backslash escapes for special characters and Unicode code points), and a closing double-quote. Returns Just of the decoded payload on success or Nothing on malformed input.
Total. The inverse of showString.
metadata.description: Parse a string-literal token to a plain string (Just) or Nothing on parse failure.

comments: readUint16(s) returns Just(x) where x is the uint16 parsed from s, or Nothing if s is not a valid non-negative integer literal or the parsed value is outside [0, 2^16-1].
Total.
metadata.description: Parse a string as a uint16.

comments: readUint32(s) returns Just(x) where x is the uint32 parsed from s, or Nothing if s is not a valid non-negative integer literal or the parsed value is outside [0, 2^32-1].
Total.
metadata.description: Parse a string as a uint32.

comments: readUint64(s) returns Just(x) where x is the uint64 parsed from s, or Nothing if s is not a valid non-negative integer literal or the parsed value is outside [0, 2^64-1].
Total.
metadata.description: Parse a string as a uint64.

comments: readUint8(s) returns Just(x) where x is the uint8 parsed from s, or Nothing if s is not a valid non-negative integer literal or the parsed value is outside [0, 255].
Total.
metadata.description: Parse a string as a uint8.

comments: showBigint(x) returns the canonical decimal representation of x: an optional leading minus sign followed by decimal digits. No leading zeros are produced (except for the value 0 itself, which renders as "0").
Total. The inverse of readBigint.
metadata.description: Render a bigint as a string.

comments: showBoolean(true) = "true"; showBoolean(false) = "false".
Total. The inverse of readBoolean.
metadata.description: Render a boolean as a string.

comments: showDecimal(x) returns the canonical decimal representation of x as a string. The exact form (use of scientific notation, trailing zeros, etc.) is host-defined but always round-trips with readDecimal.
Total.
metadata.description: Render a decimal as a string.

comments: showFloat32(x) returns a string representation of x. Finite values use the shortest decimal that round-trips through readFloat32 back to x. Special values render as "NaN", "Infinity", or "-Infinity" (capitalization is host-defined).
Total.
metadata.description: Render a float32 as a string.

comments: showFloat64(x) returns a string representation of x. Finite values use the shortest decimal that round-trips through readFloat64 back to x. Special values render as "NaN", "Infinity", or "-Infinity" (capitalization is host-defined).
Total.
metadata.description: Render a float64 as a string.

comments: showInt16(x) returns the canonical decimal representation of x: an optional leading minus sign followed by decimal digits.
Total. The inverse of readInt16.
metadata.description: Render an int16 as a string.

comments: showInt32(x) returns the canonical decimal representation of x: an optional leading minus sign followed by decimal digits.
Total. The inverse of readInt32.
metadata.description: Render an int32 as a string.

comments: showInt64(x) returns the canonical decimal representation of x: an optional leading minus sign followed by decimal digits.
Total. The inverse of readInt64.
metadata.description: Render an int64 as a string.

comments: showInt8(x) returns the canonical decimal representation of x: an optional leading minus sign followed by decimal digits.
Total. The inverse of readInt8.
metadata.description: Render an int8 as a string.

comments: showString(s) returns a Haskell-syntax string literal token representing s: an opening double-quote, the characters of s with special characters escaped (backslash sequences for control characters, quotes, and non-printable Unicode code points), and a closing double-quote.
Total. The inverse of readString.
metadata.description: Render a string as a string-literal token (escaped and quoted).

comments: showUint16(x) returns the canonical decimal representation of x.
Total. The inverse of readUint16.
metadata.description: Render a uint16 as a string.

comments: showUint32(x) returns the canonical decimal representation of x.
Total. The inverse of readUint32.
metadata.description: Render a uint32 as a string.

comments: showUint64(x) returns the canonical decimal representation of x.
Total. The inverse of readUint64.
metadata.description: Render a uint64 as a string.

comments: showUint8(x) returns the canonical decimal representation of x.
Total. The inverse of readUint8.
metadata.description: Render a uint8 as a string.

comments: stringToBinary(s) encodes s as a UTF-8 byte sequence and returns the result as binary data.
Total.
metadata.description: Convert a UTF-8 string to binary data.

comments: uint16ToBigint(x) returns the bigint with the same numeric value as x. The conversion is exact and lossless.
Total.
metadata.description: Convert a uint16 to a bigint.

comments: uint32ToBigint(x) returns the bigint with the same numeric value as x. The conversion is exact and lossless.
Total.
metadata.description: Convert a uint32 to a bigint.

comments: uint64ToBigint(x) returns the bigint with the same numeric value as x. The conversion is exact and lossless.
Total.
metadata.description: Convert a uint64 to a bigint.

comments: uint8ToBigint(x) returns the bigint with the same numeric value as x. The conversion is exact and lossless.
Total.
metadata.description: Convert a uint8 to a bigint.
Primitives in the hydra.lib.literals module.
